title: Gardening - Spacing charts tell you to give each plant room. For these eight vegetables, that's the wrong move. They produce more when they're crowded — not less.  The stress of competition triggers them to fruit harder, bolt slower, and concentrate flavor instead of wasting energy on foliage.  🌱 The tight-spacing list:  - Lettuce — plant at four-inch spacing and harvest tender baby leaves before they bolt. Wide spacing grows big heads that turn bitter before you pick them  - Radish — sow dense,
